name: IRC_REQ_SEC_PROFILES
description: Requisition Security Profile table - Each record stores conditions used to generate instance set based on requisition related objects.

columns.Name: REQ_SECURITY_PROFILE_ID
columns.Comments: System generated primary key column.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: NAME
columns.Comments: Name for the requisition security profile.

columns.Name: ENABLED_FLAG
columns.Comments: This field represents whether a requisition security profile is enabled or disabled. When it is enabled, it can be associated with a role.

columns.Name: SECURE_BY_ORGANIZATION_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option allows users to restrict requisition security profile access based on organization.

columns.Name: SECURE_BY_LOCATION_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option allows users to restrict requisition security profile access based on location.

columns.Name: SECURE_BY_JOB_FAMILY_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option allows users to restrict requisition security profile access based on job family.

columns.Name: SECURE_BY_JOB_FUNCTION_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option allows users to restrict requisition security profile access based on job function.

columns.Name: SECURE_BY_REC_TYPE_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option allows users to restrict requisition security profile access based on recruitment type.

columns.Name: SECURE_BY_AOR_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option allows users to restrict requisition security profiles based on areas of responsibility.

columns.Name: SECURE_BY_TM_TYPE_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option allows users to restrict requisition security profiles based on team member type.

columns.Name: ACCESS_SUBORDINATE_REQ_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option allows users to access requisition seen by the subordinates

columns.Name: RESPONSIBILITY_TYPE
columns.Comments: Responsibility type for areas of responsibility.

columns.Name: VIEW_ALL_FLAG
columns.Comments: This option provides unrestricted security access to the specific requisition security profile.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
